start_2.speaker: greta
start_2.text: You've been good to me — the gold, the rooms, not judging when I sell information to both sides. So I'm going to tell you something that could get me killed. Because if I don't tell someone, I'll drown in it.

the_secret.speaker: greta
the_secret.text: The Hollow Hand uses my inn as a waypoint for smuggled goods. Weapons, alchemical supplies, forged documents — it all comes through my cellar. I take a cut for the storage and for keeping quiet.

the_secret_2.speaker: greta
the_secret_2.text: But here's the thing — I've been skimming. Not a lot. A crate here, a pouch there. Enough to build a nest egg in case this village finally dies and I need to run. The Guild doesn't know. If they find out...

the_secret_3.speaker: greta
the_secret_3.text: I've been at this for six months. I have enough stashed to disappear — or to buy protection. But recently... I found something in one of the shipments that scared me more than the Guild ever could.

the_reveal.speaker: greta
the_reveal.text: Nightcap root. Three pounds of it, packed in an unmarked crate marked for pickup by someone using the code name 'Ash.' The Guild smuggled the poison that's killing this village. Whether they know it or not.

options.text: This is powerful information, Greta. And powerful information has a price.
Your secret is safe with me. No one needs to know about the skimming.
"Ash." That's a Church name. Greta, I need you to watch Brother Maren. Every visitor, every delivery.
You're in the perfect position — you see everything, hear everything. Work with me. Officially.

blackmail_path.speaker: greta
blackmail_path.text: I tell you my deepest secret and you hold it over me? I should have known. Everyone in this village is a vulture — they just wear different feathers.

blackmail_2.speaker: greta
blackmail_2.text: Fine. What do you want? Gold? I have about two hundred saved. Information? I know where every skeleton in Ashvale is buried. Just name your price and get out of my inn.

options.text: I want every secret you've collected. Every piece of leverage. Written down.
...I'm sorry. That was wrong. I shouldn't have said that.

blackmail_gold.speaker: greta
blackmail_gold.text: Six months of stealing, a lifetime of risk, and you take it in a sentence. Here. Take it and leave. And don't come back expecting a warm welcome.

blackmail_secrets.speaker: greta
blackmail_secrets.text: That's smarter than gold. Fine. I'll write it all down — Ashworth's debts, Fenrick's real handler, who visits Maren at night, the guard captain's gambling problem, all of it. Give me until morning.

blackmail_secrets_2.speaker: greta
blackmail_secrets_2.text: But know this: information is a weapon, and you just armed yourself with enough to start a war. If any of this traces back to me, I'll deny everything and disappear. And I'm very good at disappearing.

blackmail_relent.speaker: greta
blackmail_relent.text: ...You mean that? Because I've had people apologize right before twisting the knife. But your eyes... you look like you actually regret it. That's a first in this village.

blackmail_relent_2.speaker: greta
blackmail_relent_2.text: Tell you what. I'll still help you — because if someone is using the Guild's smuggling routes to poison this village, that puts ME at risk too. The codename 'Ash' — start there. That's a gift. Don't make me regret it.

silence_path.speaker: greta
silence_path.text: You... thank you. I've been carrying this alone for months. Every time Fenrick walks through that door, I think today is the day they find out.

silence_2.speaker: greta
silence_2.text: I owe you. And Greta always pays her debts. The nightcap root shipment — I still have the delivery manifest hidden under a loose board in the cellar. It has the origin point: a town called Cinderfell, two days east. Church territory.

silence_3.speaker: greta
silence_3.text: And one more thing — free of charge. Three nights ago, I saw someone at the well after midnight. Hooded, moving carefully. They poured something into the water and left by the chapel road. I couldn't see the face, but the robes... they looked like Church vestments.

spy_maren_path.speaker: greta
spy_maren_path.text: "Ash." You think it's him? ...It fits, doesn't it? The timing, the Church connection, the way he watches everyone like he's cataloging sins.

spy_maren_2.speaker: greta
spy_maren_2.text: He comes in here twice a week. Orders water, sits in the corner, and watches. I always thought he was just pious. But if he's the one picking up nightcap root from my cellar...

spy_maren_3.speaker: greta
spy_maren_3.text: I'll do it. I'll keep a log — who he talks to, when he leaves, what he orders. And I'll check the cellar for any new unmarked crates. If 'Ash' makes another pickup, I'll know.

options.text: If you catch him picking up a crate, don't confront him. Just note the time and tell me.
Better yet — mark the next nightcap crate with chalk dust. We'll follow whoever picks it up.
Replace the nightcap root with something harmless. If the poisoner uses it and the water stays clean, we know it's them.

spy_instructions_passive.speaker: greta
spy_instructions_passive.text: Passive observation. Smart — no risk to me, and the target doesn't know they're being watched. I'll have a report for you within three days. Check back then.

spy_instructions_active.speaker: greta
spy_instructions_active.text: Chalk dust. Simple, effective. Their hands, their robes, their trail — all marked. I like how you think. It'll be done by tonight. The next pickup will light up like a signal fire.

spy_instructions_sabotage.speaker: greta
spy_instructions_sabotage.text: Replace it? With what? ...Actually, I have dried huskweed that looks identical to nightcap root. Same texture, same color, but completely inert. If someone uses it and the water stays clean, that's proof they were the poisoner.

spy_sabotage_2.speaker: greta
spy_sabotage_2.text: But there's a catch — if the poisoner tests the root before using it, they'll know it's been swapped. And they'll know someone in the supply chain did it. The suspicion falls on me. Is that a risk you're willing to take with MY life?

options.text: It's worth it. One day of risk to identify the poisoner and save the whole village.
You're right. Too dangerous for you. Stick to passive observation.

sabotage_confirmed.speaker: greta
sabotage_confirmed.text: Fine. I'll make the swap tonight. But if this goes sideways, I'm disappearing. No goodbyes, no forwarding address. You'll find nothing but a cold inn and an empty cellar. Understood?

informant_path.speaker: greta
informant_path.text: Officially? You mean like... a partnership? Not just selling gossip for coin, but actually working together toward something?

informant_2.speaker: greta
informant_2.text: I've survived twenty years in this business by never committing to a side. The moment you pick a team, you make enemies of everyone else. But this village is dying, and sitting on the fence while it burns feels worse than picking wrong.

options.text: Full partnership. You report to me everything you see and hear. I protect you from the fallout.
Just the critical stuff — anything about the poisoner, the Guild's plans, or Maren's activities.

informant_full.speaker: greta
informant_full.text: All right. Full partnership. Here's what I know right now: the Guild is planning something big within the week. Fenrick's been meeting with someone from outside Ashvale — a woman with a scar across her throat. I've never seen her before.

informant_full_2.speaker: greta
informant_full_2.text: Brother Maren receives sealed letters by carrier crow every third night. He burns them immediately after reading. And Lord Ashworth's steward tried to buy poison from me last week. Not nightcap root — something faster. I refused.

informant_selective.speaker: greta
informant_selective.text: Fine. Here's the critical stuff: the codename 'Ash' picked up another nightcap shipment two days ago. And Fenrick mentioned the 'deadline' is approaching — whatever happens, it happens before the new moon. That's five days from now.

informant_end.speaker: greta
informant_end.text: I'll keep my ears open. Come back to the inn any evening and I'll have an update. And stranger? Don't let me down. I'm betting my life on you being one of the good ones.
